Dr. Eugene Kroll

Diploma, Molecular Biotechnology, St Petersburg Institute of Technology, 1990.

Ph.D. Molecular Biology and Genetics, the Johns Hopkins University School of Medicine in Baltimore and the Russian Academy of Sciences, 1995.

Postoctoral studies at Fred Hutchinson Cancer Research Center (1996-1999) with Lee Hartwell and Steven Friend.

Joined the Molecular Sciences Institute in March 2000.

Research experience and interests include chromosome stability in yeast and in cancer, microsatellites and other markers for genomic instability, genomic approaches for functional screening, evolution of control elements of genes, genetic control of eukaryotic adaptation to changing environments and making biological data suitable for computational analysis.
